title: A Walk in the Park
summary: A man describes his regular walk in the park. He talks about what he sees, hears, and enjoys during his time outside.
body: Every weekend I go for a walk in the park. The park is near my home. I usually go in the morning. The air is fresh and clean. I walk along a wide path. There are tall trees on both sides. Some birds are singing in the trees. I can hear them clearly. There is a small lake in the middle of the park. Ducks swim on the lake. Children play on the grass near the water. Some people sit on benches and read books. Dogs run and play with their owners. I always bring a bottle of water. I walk for about one hour. Sometimes I sit under a tree and rest. The park is very peaceful. I feel happy and relaxed after my walk. I love going to the park every week.

annotations.exampleEn: She goes for a walk every evening after dinner.
The mountain air was fresh and clean.
We cycled along a wide path through the forest.
There were flowers on both sides of the road.
I could hear birds singing in the garden.
We rowed a boat on the lake in the afternoon.
The children loved feeding the ducks by the lake.
There are wooden benches along the park path.
The village is very peaceful in the early morning.
She felt relaxed after a long bath.
The dogs ran back to their owners when called.

grammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency: usually, always, sometimes
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trạng từ tần suất (usually, always, sometimes, never) cho biết việc gì xảy ra thường xuyên như thế nào. Chúng thường đứng trước động từ chính: 'I usually go in the morning.', 'I always bring a bottle of water.', 'Sometimes I sit under a tree.' Lưu ý: 'sometimes' có thể đứng đầu câu.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Adverbs of frequency (usually, always, sometimes, never) tell us how often something happens. They usually go before the main verb: 'I usually go in the morning.', 'I always bring a bottle of water.', 'Sometimes I sit under a tree.' Note that 'sometimes' can also appear at the start of a sentence.

grammarNotes.title: There is / There are – Describing a place
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'There is' với danh từ số ít và 'There are' với danh từ số nhiều để mô tả những gì có mặt ở một nơi. Ví dụ: 'There is a small lake in the middle of the park.' / 'There are tall trees on both sides.' Đây là cấu trúc rất thường gặp khi mô tả địa điểm.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'There is' with a singular noun and 'There are' with a plural noun to describe what exists in a place. Examples: 'There is a small lake in the middle of the park.' / 'There are tall trees on both sides.' This structure is very common when describing places.

translationVi: Mỗi cuối tuần tôi đi dạo trong công viên. Công viên ở gần nhà tôi. Tôi thường đi vào buổi sáng. Không khí trong lành và sạch sẽ. Tôi đi dọc theo một con đường rộng. Có những cây cao ở cả hai bên. Một số con chim đang hót trên cây. Tôi có thể nghe chúng rõ ràng. Có một cái hồ nhỏ ở giữa công viên. Những con vịt bơi trên hồ. Trẻ em chơi trên bãi cỏ gần mặt nước. Một số người ngồi trên ghế dài và đọc sách. Những con chó chạy và chơi cùng chủ của chúng. Tôi luôn mang theo một chai nước. Tôi đi bộ khoảng một tiếng đồng hồ. Đôi khi tôi ngồi dưới gốc cây và nghỉ ngơi. Công viên rất yên bình. Tôi cảm thấy vui vẻ và thư giãn sau khi đi dạo. Tôi thích đến công viên mỗi tuần.

questions.prompt: When does the narrator go for a walk?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Every weekend I go for a walk in the park.'
questions.hint: How often and when does he go to the park?

questions.explanation: The passage says 'The park is near my home.'
questions.hint: How far does he have to travel to reach the park?

questions.prompt: What time of day does he usually go?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I usually go in the morning.'
questions.hint: What time does the passage mention?

questions.prompt: What can he hear in the trees?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Some birds are singing in the trees.'
questions.hint: What animals are making sounds in the trees?

options: In the middle of the park
questions.explanation: The passage says 'There is a small lake in the middle of the park.'
questions.hint: Where exactly in the park is the lake?

questions.explanation: The passage says 'Ducks swim on the lake.'
questions.hint: What birds are on the lake?

options: On the grass near the water
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Children play on the grass near the water.'
questions.hint: What surface do the children play on, and where is it?

questions.prompt: What do some people do on the benches?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Some people sit on benches and read books.'
questions.hint: What activity do people on the benches do?

questions.prompt: What does the narrator always bring to the park?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I always bring a bottle of water.'
questions.hint: What essential item does he always take with him?

questions.prompt: How long does the narrator walk?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I walk for about one hour.'

questions.prompt: How does the narrator feel after his walk?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'I feel happy and relaxed after my walk.'
questions.hint: What two positive feelings does he mention?

cloze.template: Every weekend I go for a walk in the {{b1}}. The park is near my home. I usually go in the {{b2}}. The air is fresh and clean. I walk along a wide path. There are tall trees on both {{b3}}. Some birds are {{b4}} in the trees. I can hear them clearly. There is a small lake in the middle of the park. {{b5}} swim on the lake. Children play on the grass near the water. Some people sit on {{b6}} and read books. Dogs run and play with their {{b7}}. I always bring a bottle of {{b8}}. I walk for about one {{b9}}. Sometimes I sit under a tree and rest. The park is very {{b10}}. I feel happy and relaxed after my walk.

blanks.explanation: The whole passage describes a walk in 'the park'.
'I usually go in the morning' — early morning is the time for his walk.
'On both sides' means on the left and right of the path.
'Some birds are singing in the trees' — the narrator can hear them.
'Ducks swim on the lake' — ducks are common birds found on park lakes.
'Sit on benches' — park benches are outdoor seats for visitors.
'With their owners' — dogs play alongside the people who own them.
'A bottle of water' is essential for staying hydrated during exercise.
'About one hour' is the length of his walk.
'The park is very peaceful' — quiet and calm, perfect for relaxation.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says he feels happy and relaxed after walking in the park. Why do you think being in nature can make people feel better? Do you think everyone has access to a park like this?
